chemicals.description: Substances that cause gastrointestinal upset and skin irritation.
chemicals.name: Unidentified Irritants

description: Dahlia (Dahlia spp.) is a common garden flowering plant that is mildly toxic to cats. The plant contains irritant compounds — including sesquiterpene lactones — primarily in the leaves and stem. These substances cause gastrointestinal irritation and, with skin contact, mild dermatitis.
name: Dahlia
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: sesquiterpene lactones and unidentified irritant components
Mechanism: direct mucosal and GI irritation
Symptoms: vomiting, diarrhea, mild drooling; skin contact may cause localised redness or itching
All above-ground parts carry some irritant potential; flower petals are least concentrated
Symptoms are usually mild and self-limiting, but contact your vet if your cat ingests a large quantity

symptoms.name: Dermatitis
symptoms.notes: Skin irritation upon contact or ingestion.

treatments.description: Bath with mild soap to remove sap/irritants.
treatments.name: Dermal Cleansing

treatments.description: Fluids and anti-emetics for GI symptoms.
treatments.name: Supportive Care
